steps.title: Simplify the function
steps.content:
We have $c(r) = \frac{3}{8}(2\pi r)$. Multiplying the constants:
$$c(r) = \frac{3\pi r}{4}$$
This is a linear function with slope $\frac{3\pi}{4}$.

steps.title: Find k using the rate of change
steps.content:
For a linear function, when the radius increases by 18 cm, the change in $c$ is:
$$\Delta c = \frac{3\pi}{4} \cdot 18 = \frac{27\pi}{2}$$
Since this change equals $k\pi$, we have $k\pi = \frac{27\pi}{2}$, so $k = \frac{27}{2} = 13.5$.

Equivalent forms accepted: 27/2, 13.5.
